title: astragalus globiflorus var. andalanicus
intro: Astragalus globiflorus var. andalanicus, also known as Andalayan milk vetch, is a variety of the Astragalus globiflorus plant, a type of legume that belongs to the pea family. It is used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic properties, which help the body to resist stress, and is believed to have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fatigue, improving cardiovascular function, and supporting the immune system. Astragalus globiflorus var. andalanicus is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, capsules, and extracts, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
